$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; queryId + artisanpack/postPreview added to usesContext for editor-preview parity. attribute schema and supports carried verbatim.

Previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit. Reads the stamped _resolvedTermsLabel attribute, then the resolved post's terms[taxonomy] map from artisanpack/postPreview context, then the live page entity's _preview.terms envelope. Upstream edit.js queries the terms collection through @wordpress/core-data, which the post editor doesn't expose.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced server-side from stamped _resolvedTerms* attributes.
Bidirectional core/post-terms ↔ artisanpack/post-terms block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

Single v1 entry ported to TypeScript: migrates the legacy top-level textAlign attribute into the canonical block-support shape via the vendored migrate-text-align helper. Save returns null — block is server-rendered.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via createEntityPlaceholderEdit rather than upstream edit.js — the core-data shim does not expose the terms collection to the post editor, so the upstream entity-querying edit renders empty.
post-terms is server-rendered: the front-end markup is produced by the Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolvedTerms* attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/post-terms during the V1 rollout.
